title: Vultar

{{Quote|There they discovered ancient technology indicating that the system's planets were artificial constructs, the likely creations of the alien Architects who had built the Corellian system. The dark-siders harnessed the machines (including the extraordinary Cosmic Turbine) but could not control them, and soon annihilated themselves along with the entire planetary system.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC" />}}
'''Vultar''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE VultarCataclysm">{{CSWECite|III|314|Vultar Cataclysm}}</ref> located in the [[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]],<ref name="NEC" /> a part of the [[Arrowhead]] region in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA Vultar" /> Evidence pointed to the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]],<ref name="GH39">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 39: The Third Great Schism}}</ref> an advanced [[Species/Legends|species]] that disappeared from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in [[30,000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> having used a set of devices,<ref name="GH39" /> including<ref name="CSWE CosmicTurbine">{{CSWECite|I|148|Cosmic Turbine}}</ref> the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulation machine known as the [[Cosmic Turbine]],<ref name="TEA" /> to construct the Vultar [[Star system/Legends|system]],<ref name="GH39" /> including its planets.<ref name="NEC" />

In [[4250 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> when a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] activated those same devices, including the Cosmic Turbine, in order to use them against their enemy, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], a [[Vultar Cataclysm|catastrophic accident]] instead [[Mundicide|destroyed]] the machines, the [[darksider]]s, and the entire Vultar system,<ref name="GH39" /> leaving behind the [[Vultar Nebula]].<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> [[Millennium/Legends|Millennia]] later, the fate of the Vultar system's planets was recorded in a [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|document]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]], published in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]] was the first source to mention the planets of the Vultar system.<ref name="NEC" /> The planet Vultar was subsequently identified in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE VultarCataclysm" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Vultar system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square L-10.<ref name="TEA Vultar" />
